lede: A look at November 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Mason Ramsey and Aditya Roy Kapur, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Artemis program
events.desc: Artemis Program: NASA launches Artemis 1 on the first flight of the Space Launch System, the start of the program's future missions to the moon.

events.title: 2002–2004 SARS outbreak
events.desc: The first cases of the 2002–2004 SARS outbreak are traced to Foshan, Guangdong Province, China.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 3603
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 3603 crashes during landing at Norilsk Airport, killing 99.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: U.S. president Richard Nixon signs the Trans-Alaska Pipeline Authorization Act into law, authorizing the construction of the Alaska Pipeline.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 2230
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 2230 crashes near Koltsovo Airport, killing 107.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In response to the leveling of Coventry by the German Luftwaffe two days before, the Royal Air Force bombs Hamburg.

events.title: George Metesky
events.desc: New York City's "Mad Bomber" George Metesky places his first bomb at a Manhattan office building used by Consolidated Edison.

events.title: Siege of Lucknow
events.desc: Second relief of Lucknow: Twenty-four Victoria Crosses are awarded, the most in a single day.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British and Hessian units capture Fort Washington from the Patriots.

events.title: Gustavus Adolphus
events.desc: King Gustavus Adolphus of Sweden was killed at the Battle of Lützen during the Thirty Years' War.

deaths.text: Juan Joseph, American football player and coach

observances.text: Christian feast day: Gertrude the Great (Roman Catholic Church)
